what: A written artifact — an explanation, a design essay, a ruling — that yields no code and is proved by human judgment.
why: Not all work compiles to code; some of it is understanding made shareable (Telos 2–3). A concept-piece still gets proved and still feeds back — but its proof is review, not a tester run. This exists so the workflow rides on node-class, needing zero new artifact types: the same ticket + VALIDATION schema records a human-proved node.
prove_gate: quorum signature gate — N human reviewers read and sign. Recorded as a VALIDATION: method = 'review by N readers', caller = the reviewers, evidence = their restated-back understanding.
extra_gates: none beyond the quorum gate in v0.
kicks_back_on: a reviewer rejects → red → routes back to the point of creation exactly like a red build (CP2).
provenance: first concept-piece: IntentionBasedDesignForHumans.md, born + reviewed 2026-07-15. Note: verdict (reviewers) and seal (notary) are different hands here — not a code-proof.
